package: GoalOS Signoff Pro — Public-Private Proof Boundary Lab

doctrine.headline: Do not publish private intelligence. Publish proof commitments.
doctrine.law: Private execution stays private; public proof remains verifiable.
doctrine.rule: A public proof page must reveal only the minimum evidence required to coordinate trust.
doctrine.claimBoundary: Synthetic browser-local demonstration only. It does not publish private customer data, execute a real audit, certify production readiness, move value, connect a wallet, or perform a Mainnet transaction.

lanes.label: Private execution
lanes.description: Raw prompts, traces, customer files, credentials, and privileged evaluator notes remain outside the public artifact.

lanes.label: Redaction pass
lanes.description: Sensitive values are removed; only hashes, reason codes, schema names, and public-safe summaries advance.

lanes.label: Public proof
lanes.description: Commitments, roots, attestations, proof packet hashes, cost/risk summaries, and claim boundaries become inspectable.

lanes.label: Selection record
lanes.description: Only a proof-cleared record can influence Chronicle memory, future routing, reuse, or settlement readiness.

scenarios.label: AI research acceptance
scenarios.mission: Review an AI-generated research memo without publishing private drafts or reviewer workpapers.

scenarios.label: Software delivery review
scenarios.mission: Convert a software delivery into a public-safe proof page while private repository traces remain scoped.

scenarios.label: Procurement proof room
scenarios.mission: Show why a vendor recommendation is reviewable without exposing commercial details.

scenarios.label: Safety escalation boundary
scenarios.mission: Preserve a high-impact evidence record while refusing to publish sensitive traces or grant authority.
